formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Approximately half of Intermediate-Density Lipoproteins (IDLs) are removed by hepatocytes via apoE/low-density lipoprotein receptor–mediated endocytosis; the remaining portion is metabolized by hepatic lipase into low-density lipoprotein (LDL). This dual pathway represents the primary metabolic fates of IDL in circulation. 

Final Answer:\boxed{C}

The two main fates of IDL in plasma are its removal by the liver via apolipoprotein E-receptor mediated endocytosis and transformation into LDL by hepatic lipase—each occurring in nearly equal proportions. These parallel mechanisms account for all known post-secretion processing of IDL.  

Final Answer:\boxed{C}. Answer choice: C
